project.name: Heady™ Latent Operating System
project.vision: A 46-year autonomous AI operating system that stores executable potential as AST nodes in pgvector, materializes code on-demand, and operates as a self-healing multi-swarm civilization.
project.architecture: Zero-Repo Liquid Architecture — code only exists during compilation. 18 swarms, 31+ bees, 742 aspirational tasks.

coreModules.heady-manager: Express.js server — entry point, bootstraps all services, serves all 9 domains via domain routing
coreModules.vector-memory: pgvector-backed 3D vector memory with PCA-lite projection, 8-octant zone mapping, Fibonacci sharding
coreModules.domain-router: Maps hostnames to UI modules — 22 hostname entries, 12 unique UI modules
coreModules.site-renderer: Dynamic HTML generation with Sacred Geometry animations and chat widgets
coreModules.auto-projection: Pre-renders all 9 sites on boot, caches in RAM, pushes to Cloudflare KV edge
coreModules.vault-boot: Encrypted credential vault — AES-256-GCM, decrypts at boot, projects into process.env
coreModules.swarm-matrix: 18-swarm, 31-bee runtime registry with status tracking and activation APIs
coreModules.aspirational-registry: 742 unified aspirational tasks from 14 sources, mapped to swarms
coreModules.hologram-bee: On-demand AST-to-edge compiler — replaces CI/CD entirely
coreModules.context-weaver-bee: Zero-file memory assembler — builds ephemeral LLM context from AST nodes
coreModules.projection-engine: Manages deployment targets: Cloud Run, Cloudflare Edge, Colab GPU, HuggingFace
coreModules.governance: Perfect Governance — cryptographic stamping, proof chains, immutable audit trail
coreModules.structured-logger: Pino-based JSON logging with OpenTelemetry correlation IDs
coreModules.health-registry: Service health monitoring with circuit breakers
coreModules.continuous-embedder: Auto-embeds all project data into 3D vector memory

beeSystem.description: Bees are autonomous micro-services that perform specific tasks. Each bee has a class, role, status (ACTIVE/STANDBY/SLEEPER), and swarm assignment.

stack.gpu: Google Colab Pro+ (ML training, batch inference)
stack.cicd: GitHub Actions (5-phase: security → validate → Cloud Run → HF → Edge)
stack.ai: Multi-model: Claude, GPT-5.4, Gemini, Groq, Perplexity, HuggingFace
stack.protocols: MCP (Model Context Protocol) for tool exposure

fileStructure.src/bees/: Autonomous bee agents (one file per bee)

patterns: Zero-Repo Architecture — code stored as AST JSON in pgvector, materialized on-demand
Liquid Architecture — every service is a projection that can be materialized anywhere
RAM-First Operations — all ops in vector space, external stores are projections
Perfect Governance — every mutation is cryptographically stamped and auditable
Auto-Success Pipeline — all deployments self-verify and never fail the CI/CD
Sacred Geometry — UI design system based on golden ratio, Fibonacci sequences
Swarm Intelligence — 18 swarms collaborate autonomously via orchestration protocol
Remote-Only Backups — no local storage, backups in Cloudflare R2 / GCS Coldline
rebuildInstructions:
You are rebuilding the Heady™ Latent OS from scratch.

CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS:
1. Each module must be its own file — NO monolithic files
2. Every bee gets its own file in src/bees/
3. Every service gets its own file in src/services/
4. Use the EXACT same API endpoints and route structure
5. Implement proper error handling with structured logging
6. Use pgvector for vector memory (1536-dim embeddings)
7. Implement the vault system (AES-256-GCM encrypted credentials)
8. Build the domain router to serve all 9 domains from one Express server
9. Implement the swarm matrix with all 18 swarms and 31 bees
10. Build the HologramBee (AST-to-edge compiler) and ContextWeaverBee (memory assembler)
11. Sacred Geometry CSS animations on all site renders
12. Health checks on every service with circuit breaker pattern
13. Dockerfile must be multi-stage with non-root user
14. GitHub Actions CI/CD with security scanning

OUTPUT FORMAT:
- Create a complete, runnable Node.js project
- Include package.json with all dependencies
- Include Dockerfile
- Include .github/workflows/deploy.yml
- Include README.md with architecture documentation
- Every file should be production-quality with JSDoc comments
